career_angle: If you work in symbolic AI or formal verification, Axplorer is worth a weekend install to see if the pattern-discovery approach generalizes beyond combinatorics. Don't pivot your career around it -- wait for peer-reviewed results on problems beyond Turán four-cycles before treating this as a real paradigm shift.

factors.rationale: MIT Technology Review is tier-1 tech journalism with editorial standards, not a blog repost
One verified result (Turán four-cycles via PatternBoost) extrapolated into sweeping claims about Axplorer with zero new benchmarks or peer review
Free tool that runs locally is good in principle, but no independent verification that Axplorer reproduces PatternBoost's results or extends them
Startup launch-day exclusive with only the founder and their own research scientist as sources -- textbook earned media placement
AI-for-math is a real field and DARPA expMath is real, but no independent mathematicians quoted validating this specific tool
Names real prior work and DARPA program but 'discover mathematical patterns that could unlock solutions' is vaporware-grade vague on what Axplorer actually does differently
Charton's Meta research is legit, but Axiom Math is brand new with zero shipped results of its own
